markets.no_sub_title: Washington wins 2nd half
markets.rules_primary: If Washington is the winner of the second half of regulation time in the Golden State vs Washington professional basketball game originally scheduled for Mar 16, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: The following market refers to the team that scores the most points during the specified half. Only points scored during the specified half of play count towards this market. If both teams score the same amount of points during the half, all team strikes resolve to No and the Tie strike resolves to Yes. If this game is postponed or delayed, the market will remain open and close after the rescheduled game has finished (within two weeks).

markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the event occurs.
markets.no_sub_title: Paolo Banchero
markets.rules_primary: If Paolo Banchero records a Triple Double in the Orlando at Atlanta professional basketball game originally scheduled for Mar 16, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: Statistics are considered final at the conclusion of the specified time period of the basketball game. Statistics that count towards a triple double include points, rebounds, assists, blocks, and steals. Any combination of 10+ in 3 or more of these statistics results in a triple double. Corrections made after the game ends will not affect resolution. Statistics recorded during both regulation time and overtime count unless the time period specifies otherwise (e.g., "regulation only"). If the game is cancelled before starting, markets resolve to last fair price. If cancelled after starting and not resumed within 24 hours, markets resolve to last fair price except for thresholds already reached. If an entity (player or team) withdraws before the game, markets on that entity resolve to last fair price. If a player is injured, retires, or is disqualified during the game after playing at least one second, markets settle based on end-of-game statistics.

markets.rules_secondary: Statistics are considered final at the conclusion of the specified time period of the basketball game. Statistics that count towards a double double include points, rebounds, assists, blocks, and steals. Any combination of 10+ in 2 or more of these statistics results in a double double. Corrections made after the game ends will not affect resolution. Statistics recorded during both regulation time and overtime count unless the time period specifies otherwise (e.g., "regulation only"). If the game is cancelled before starting, markets resolve to last fair price. If cancelled after starting and not resumed within 24 hours, markets resolve to last fair price except for thresholds already reached. If an entity (player or team) withdraws before the game, markets on that entity resolve to last fair price. If a player is injured, retires, or is disqualified during the game after playing at least one second, markets settle based on end-of-game statistics.
